summary: Re-tested parseSayi decimal fix and personeller endpoint in MotorinVerme.js. All checks PASS. Uploaded xlsx with mixed values: 250.5, 75.25, 1234.56 (raw decimals), '1.234,56' (TR thousands+decimal), '1,5' (TR small decimal), 300 (integer). PREVIEW row now shows 250.5/75.25/1234.56/1234.56/1.5/300 (NOT 2505/7525/123456). After 'Kaydı Oluştur', records modal (GET /api/motorin-verme-uploads/{id}/records via API used by frontend) shows the same persisted values, confirming both client parsing and backend persistence retain decimals. Turkish-formatted '1.234,56' parsed to 1234.56 and '1,5' parsed to 1.5 — i.e. comma branch of parseSayi works. /api/personeller now returns 200 (72 personnel) — no more 404. Inline edit (changed first row miktar to 999.99 and clicked record-save), edit-record-modal open, records-modal open/close, and 'Yüklenmiş Excel Dosyaları' list all functional. Backend not re-tested (already 100% in iter 1).
critical_code_review_comments: MotorinVerme.js still 1431+ lines with three nested modals; consider splitting into <UploadsList>, <ExcelPreview>, <RecordsModal>, <EditRecordModal> for maintainability (non-blocking).

parseSayi heuristic is correct for the common cases (number, '250.5', '1.234,56', '1,5', '300') but will still mis-parse rare ambiguous values like '1.234' (no comma, no fraction part) — currently parsed as 1.234 which is fine for litre values; pure thousands-separator integers without decimals would be unusual in this context, so acceptable.
Consider also passing raw:true to XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json so numeric cells arrive as JS numbers and skip parseSayi entirely (defense-in-depth).
seed_data_creation: Created plaka TEST34BULK99 (POST /api/araclar) and uploaded test_motorin_decimal.xlsx with 6 rows of mixed decimal formats. Cleaned up: deleted the upload+records (DELETE /api/motorin-verme-uploads/{id}?delete_records=true) and the vehicle (DELETE /api/araclar/{id}) after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Decimal bug from iter1 is fully fixed. parseSayi in /app/frontend/src/pages/MotorinVerme.js (lines 79-91) now: if value is number → return as-is; if string contains comma → strip dots + replace comma with dot (TR format); otherwise parseFloat directly (preserves '250.5' style). fetchPersoneller uses /api/personeller (line 187) which returns 200. To re-verify quickly: build xlsx with openpyxl containing 250.5 in Miktar column, login via /api/auth/login (NOT /api/login), navigate to /motorin-verme-giris, choose BİMS POMPA → Excel mode → upload → preview must show 250.5.
rca_of_the_issue: RESOLVED. Original bug: parseSayi unconditionally did s.replace(/\./g,'').replace(',','.') turning '250.5' into 2505. Fix in place: only strip dots when comma is present (Turkish thousands-separator case); else use parseFloat(s) directly so plain decimal strings like '250.5' parse correctly. Verified end-to-end through UI preview and persisted GET. No more /api/personeller 404 — fetchPersoneller now hits the correct path.
